text: Daytona is compatible with DeepAgents at the sandbox capability level because it exposes isolated sandboxes with process, filesystem, lifecycle, and network controls.
DeepAgents should integrate Daytona through the existing DisposableSandbox contract instead of adding a parallel abstraction.
Daytona session command log streaming is the strongest candidate for DeepAgents spawn because it can stream stdout and stderr while a command is still running.
A Daytona-specific high-level wrapper should default the working destination to /home/daytona rather than relying on the generic /workspace default.
Daytona can reuse DeepAgents file-event observation if the chosen Daytona image includes find and sha256sum or the wrapper ensures equivalent tools exist.
The implementation needs explicit handling for Daytona API rate limits and lifecycle rate limits because sandbox creation and lifecycle operations are throttled by tier.
DeepAgents should expose Daytona network controls as first-class options because Daytona supports allow-list and block-all egress settings with tier restrictions.
The first implementation should include an integration test that mirrors the Docker spawn test expectations against a real Daytona sandbox when DAYTONA_API_KEY is present.

Daytona documents multiple streaming surfaces relevant to DeepAgents: session command log streaming with stdout and stderr callbacks, PTY output callbacks, and an official Codex guide that streams async session command output.
